purpose: Codex-to-UniGrok MCP routing for this project

model_plane_contract.catalog_policy: Treat each plane's live catalog as authoritative. Never infer plane membership from a product name or persist a model snapshot as routing truth.
model_plane_contract.pin_policy: Pin only an exact model id present in the selected live plane catalog. A model pin selects a model, not a new credential plane.

fallback_policy.same_plane: Use when crossing the caller's selected credential or billing plane is forbidden.
fallback_policy.cross_plane: Use only when the user or caller explicitly permits bounded recovery on the other credential plane.

routes.when: Codex needs UniGrok MCP availability, adapter status, or server health.
routes.note: Use discovery with include_models=true when model routing matters; use status for current plane health. Neither check needs an inference call.

routes.when: Codex needs Grok MCP to inspect local project file names or a bounded local file.
routes.note: Prefer Codex local filesystem reads first. Use Grok only for explicit review or synthesis, and avoid sending secrets.

routes.when: Codex needs Grok MCP read-only git context.
routes.note: Prefer Codex local git commands first. Use Grok for explicit review or synthesis over bounded git context.

routes.when: The user explicitly asks @grok or asks Grok to answer a bounded question.
The user explicitly asks for Grok peer review, architecture review, or agentic Grok analysis.
The user asks for long-running Grok research or deferred Grok analysis.
routes.note: Use mode research when the live agent tool exposes it. Some provider namespaces mention deferred Grok tools; Codex must discover current UniGrok MCP tools before naming or invoking them.

do_not_answer_directly_when: The user explicitly mentions @grok as the desired reviewer
The user asks for Grok MCP status or Grok model/profile information
The user asks to compare Codex output with Grok output
